commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a diagnostic `openclaw doctor` warning for QMD-backed session search without QMD transcript export, plus memory docs and doctor tests for the session recall gates.

PR surface: Source +18, Tests +93, Docs +86. Total +197 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main's QMD doctor path returns after the binary probe, while PR head adds a warning when resolved sources include `sessions` and QMD transcript export is not enabled. I did not run the command locally because this review was constrained to read-only inspection.

target.title: fix(doctor): warn and document QMD session recall gates
target.body:
## Summary

- Add an `openclaw doctor` warning when QMD is the active memory backend and the default agent searches `sessions`, but QMD session transcript export is not enabled.
- Clarify the session transcript recall gates in memory docs:
  - session-memory must be enabled;
  - `memorySearch.sources` must include `sessions`;
  - QMD transcript export additionally requires `memory.qmd.sessions.enabled: true`;
  - session hits still obey `tools.sessions.visibility`.
- Point users to either enable `memory.qmd.sessions.enabled` or remove `sessions` from the default agent's `memorySearch.sources` when QMD session recall is not intended.
- Cover the warning, enabled, explicit-disabled, memory-only, and combined QMD-binary-warning cases in `doctor-memory-search` tests.

## Why

Related to #53550 and supersedes #80935.

The underlying QMD session recall behavior has multiple gates that are easy to miss:

1. session-memory must be enabled;
2. `memorySearch.sources` must include `sessions`;
3. when QMD is the memory backend, transcript export must also be enabled with `memory.qmd.sessions.enabled: true`;
4. returned session hits are filtered by `tools.sessions.visibility`.

This PR keeps behavior unchanged. It adds documentation plus a diagnostic-only warning for the common QMD export mismatch. It does not enable QMD session export automatically, widen transcript visibility defaults, or change indexing/search behavior.

## Validation

- `git diff --check`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.commands.config.ts src/commands/doctor-memory-search.test.ts` (46 passed)
- `corepack pnpm dlx --config.resolution-mode=highest markdownlint-cli2 --config config/markdownlint-cli2.jsonc docs/concepts/memory-qmd.md docs/concepts/memory-search.md docs/reference/memory-config.md` (0 errors)
- `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/concepts/memory-qmd.md docs/concepts/memory-search.md docs/reference/memory-config.md` (passed)

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: `openclaw doctor` now warns when QMD is the active memory backend, the default agent resolves `memorySearch.sources` with `sessions`, and QMD session transcript export is not enabled.
- **Real environment tested**: Disposable OpenClaw source checkout at PR head `27a3e91c086b425bed92eb486ed921b3f7611318` on Linux/arm64 with staged `HOME`, `OPENCLAW_HOME`, `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, staged workspace/state, and a staged `qmd` probe stub. No live gateway, live user config, live session store, or real secret-bearing environment was used.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: In the disposable checkout, I ran a one-shot Node/tsx harness that imports the current-head `doctor-memory-search` implementation and invokes the same memory-search health contribution used by `openclaw doctor --non-interactive`. The staged config set `memory.backend: "qmd"`, default-agent `memorySearch.experimental.sessionMemory: true`, `memorySearch.sources: ["memory", "sessions"]`, and intentionally omitted `memory.qmd.sessions.enabled`.
- **Evidence after fix**: Copied terminal output from the isolated run:

  ```text
  $ env -i HOME=[stage]/home OPENCLAW_HOME=[stage]/home \
      OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=[stage]/openclaw.json \
      node --import tsx [stage]/run-memory-search-health.mjs
  OpenClaw source HEAD: 27a3e91c086b425bed92eb486ed921b3f7611318
  Command: openclaw doctor --non-interactive (memory-search health contribution)

  ◇  Memory search

     QMD memory backend is configured and the default agent resolves
     memorySearch.sources with sessions,
     but QMD session transcript export is not enabled
     (memory.qmd.sessions.enabled is not true).
     Session transcript hits will not appear in QMD-backed memory search
     until QMD session export is enabled.

     Fix (pick one):
     - Enable QMD session export: openclaw config set memory.qmd.sessions.enabled true
     - Or remove sessions from the default agent's memorySearch.sources if QMD session recall is not intended.

     Verify: openclaw memory status --deep
  ```

- **Observed result after fix**: The current-head doctor memory-search contribution emitted the new QMD session-transcript export warning, including the affected gate (`memory.qmd.sessions.enabled`) and both remediation paths.
- **What was not tested**: Live gateway startup, live user configuration, real QMD indexing/search, and session transcript visibility were deliberately not exercised; this PR is diagnostic/docs-only and the proof is limited to the after-fix doctor warning path.

## Scope

Diagnostic and documentation only. No runtime behavior change to session transcript indexing, export, search, or visibility defaults.
target.author: anyech
